source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
The second phase of the 2021 Chinese Football Association Futsal Super League commenced on September 8 in Qiannan Prefecture, Guizhou Province, marking the first time the league has been held in Guizhou. The futsal field measures $40\mathrm{m}$ in length and $20\mathrm{m}$ in width, as shown in the figure. During the match, an attacking player broke through the defense $10\mathrm{m}$ from the center line and kicked the ball forward along the sideline. The motion of the ball can be considered as a uniformly decelerated linear motion on the ground with an initial velocity of $v_{1}=5\mathrm{m/s}$ and an acceleration magnitude of $a_{1}=0.5\mathrm{m/s}^{2}$. After kicking the ball, the player started chasing it with a delay of $1\mathrm{s}$, beginning from rest. His motion can be described as a uniformly accelerated linear motion with an acceleration of $a_{2}=1\mathrm{m/s}^{2}$, reaching a maximum speed of $v_{2}=6\mathrm{m/s}$, which he can maintain for an extended period.

1. At what time after the player starts chasing the football is the distance between them the greatest? What is the maximum distance?
2. How long does it take for the player to catch up to the football from the start? What is the speed of the football at that moment?

issues: Top-level image_paths and metadata.image_paths are inconsistent.

image_dependency: Question explicitly says 'as shown in the figure' and uses spatial references such as field dimensions, center line, and sideline.
An image asset exists in the registry and registry_passed=true.
multi_step: The problem involves two moving entities: a ball with uniform deceleration and a player with delayed start and accelerated motion.
The player's motion is piecewise because he accelerates from rest and then continues at a capped maximum speed of 6 m/s.
The question asks for both the time/max value of greatest separation and the later catch-up time plus ball speed, requiring multiple linked computations.
Answer requires combining initial offset, 1 s delay, relative motion, and kinematics equations.
verifiability: Expected outputs are concrete numeric quantities with units: times, distance, and speed.
The setup is a deterministic kinematics problem, so the result can be checked by equation-based solvers.
answer_text provides a finite short set of values: '3s, 11.5m' and '7s, 1m/s'.
Registry indicates question and answer are present and registry_passed=true.

risk_flags: Although requires_image=true, the text already includes substantial quantitative information, so exact image necessity may be somewhat ambiguous.
Top-level image_paths and metadata.image_paths are inconsistent.

decision_reasons: Question, answer, and primary image are all present, so core semantics are well supported.
Initial scoring is strong for image dependency, multi-step reasoning, and verifiability.
Registry passed successfully, and the available assets appear sufficient for recovery and validation.
Recorded issues are metadata consistency risks rather than missing-core-asset failures.

asset_records.source_text_snapshot:
The second phase of the 2021 Chinese Football Association Futsal Super League commenced on September 8 in Qiannan Prefecture, Guizhou Province, marking the first time the league has been held in Guizhou. The futsal field measures $40\mathrm{m}$ in length and $20\mathrm{m}$ in width, as shown in the figure. During the match, an attacking player broke through the defense $10\mathrm{m}$ from the center line and kicked the ball forward along the sideline. The motion of the ball can be considered as a uniformly decelerated linear motion on the ground with an initial velocity of $v_{1}=5\mathrm{m/s}$ and an acceleration magnitude of $a_{1}=0.5\mathrm{m/s}^{2}$. After kicking the ball, the player started chasing it with a delay of $1\mathrm{s}$, beginning from rest. His motion can be described as a uniformly accelerated linear motion with an acceleration of $a_{2}=1\mathrm{m/s}^{2}$, reaching a maximum speed of $v_{2}=6\mathrm{m/s}$, which he can maintain for an extended period.

1. At what time after the player starts chasing the football is the distance between them the greatest? What is the maximum distance?
2. How long does it take for the player to catch up to the football from the start? What is the speed of the football at that moment?

2. How long does it take for the player to catch up to the football from the start? What is the speed of the football at that moment?

open_ended_problem_variants.title: PhysReason 开放题
open_ended_problem_variants.rewritten_question_text:
A futsal field measures $40\mathrm{m}$ in length and $20\mathrm{m}$ in width, as shown in the figure. During the match, an attacking player broke through the defense $10\mathrm{m}$ from the center line and kicked the ball forward along the sideline. The motion of the ball can be considered as uniformly decelerated linear motion on the ground with an initial velocity of $v_{1}=5\mathrm{m/s}$ and an acceleration magnitude of $a_{1}=0.5\mathrm{m/s}^{2}$. After kicking the ball, the player started chasing it with a delay of $1\mathrm{s}$, beginning from rest. His motion can be described as uniformly accelerated linear motion with an acceleration of $a_{2}=1\mathrm{m/s}^{2}$, reaching a maximum speed of $v_{2}=6\mathrm{m/s}$, which he can maintain for an extended period.

1. At what time after the player starts chasing the football is the distance between them the greatest? What is the maximum distance?
2. How long does it take for the player to catch up to the football from the start? What is the speed of the football at that moment?
